data.title: Palm Oil Rebounds Strongly
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures surged near 2.0% to around 4,590 per tonne, bouncing back from losses in the prior session amid supply concerns.

Malaysia’s meteorological agency forecasts heavy rainfall in key palm-growing states until February 25, potentially disrupting harvests and transport.

Meanwhile, worker productivity is expected to decline during Ramadan and the subsequent festive season.

In Indonesia, the government is studying a biodiesel blend with 50% palm oil for 2026 and a 3% jet fuel blend.

However, weak exports capped gains, with Malaysian shipments falling 12.3%-19.9% in the first 15 days of February.

Meanwhile, India’s Solvent Extractors Association urged New Delhi to accelerate the "National Mission on Edible Oils" to boost domestic oilseed production.

On the tariff front, US President Trump is set to announce new duties on automobiles, chips, and drugs by April 2, adding to earlier tariffs.

These measures have caused persistent market distortions, including for palm oil.

data.title: HSBC 2024 profit beats estimates; announces $2 billion share buyback
data.description:
HSBC reported a 6.6% rise in annual profit on Wednesday, beating market expectations, as income withstood the impact of falling interest rates during a year in which sweeping restructuring pushed up costs.

The London-based bank reported profit before tax for 2024 of $32.3 billion. That compared with $30.3 billion a year earlier and the $31.7 billion average of analyst estimates compiled by the bank.

The Asia-focused bank also announced a new $2 billion share buyback which it plans to complete before next earnings in May.

The bank said it aimed to generate about $300 million of cost reductions in 2025, with a commitment to an annualised reduction of $1.5 billion in cost base expected by the end of 2026.

HSBC said it was aiming for a performance target of a mid-teens return on average tangible equity for each of the three years from 2025 to 2027, while noting the outlook for interest rates remains volatile and uncertain in the medium term.

The results come as CEO Georges Elhedery has embarked on costly restructuring just the outlook for lending revenue is muddied by divergence in central bank interest rate policies, with the euro zone having room to cut rates, the U.S. holding steady and Japan expected to raise.

data.title: Amlak Finance Seeks Shareholder Nod for Assets Sale
data.description:
Amlak Finance (DFM:AMLAK) called on a shareholder meeting to approve the sale of some of its investment properties, according to a Tuesday release.

The Emirati real estate financing company intends to sell the Ras Al Khor plots and other unspecified assets, pending regulatory approval.

data.title: New Zealand Shares Slip 0.1% as RBNZ Cuts Cash Rate — Market Talk
data.description: New Zealand's NZX-50 closed 0.1% lower at 13033.36, falling for a second consecutive session of narrow declines, as the country's central bank slashed the cash rate by another 50 basis points. The benchmark index gave back earlier gains as the Reserve Bank of New Zealand also warned of rising potential for international trade tensions, slower global growth and geopolitical shocks. With the cash rate at 3.75%, the RBNZ has cut by 175 bps since mid-2024. Property and aged-care stocks were mixed, while consumer-related shares were weak. Retailer Hallenstein Glasson shed 0.9% and casino operator SkyCity fell 2.0%. Pharmaceutical supplier Ebos was the worst performer, falling 4.9% after announcing the retirement of its CEO. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Crypto Has Moved Past FTX, But Still Needs 24/7 Risk Management, Brevan Howard's CIO Believes
data.description:
The crypto ecosystem has come a long way since the implosion of Sam Bankman Fried's FTX destroyed billions in investor wealth in 2023. However, the industry as a whole needs to more to become bullet proof, said TradFi experts at the "Views From Wall Street to Crypto" event held at Consensus Hong Kong on Wednesday.

"You have traditional players who have come into the space now, especially for us, most of our trading happens of exchange settlement, where you actually keep your assets on custodians while you are able to trade on exchanges," Gautam Sharma, CEO and CIO of Brevan Howard said. "So the technology has come far ahead in terms of the last 18 months since then, [but] there's more work to do."

Sharma stressed the need for 24/7 risk management, including market, counterparty, and credit risks.

Counterparty risk refers to the possibility of one party involved in a transaction failing to meet its obligation, resulting in a loss to the other party. This type of risk is higher in crypto than in traditional finance, given the absence of intermediaries such as banks or clearing houses that ensure trust and settlements, and it is a cause of concern for both directional and non-directional arbitration players.

"When we do arbitrage, the counterparty risk is the most important one," Fabio Frontini, founder of Abraxas Capital Management, said, adding that credit risk is also very important.

Frontini stressed the importance of simulating stress testing scenarios, referring to the perpetual futures market where users can lose the margin when stopped out on a trade, which is not the case in traditional markets. "It [stress testing] can be very rewarding, when done properly," Frontini added.

Mike Kuehnel, CEO of the market-making firm Flow Traders, highlighted the need to make innovation transparent to win over investor confidence and ensure "availability of data and moving liquidity without fragmentation around it."

"Getting the best price and giving you the possibility to transact whenever you want to is a key ingredient," Kuehnel added.

Liquidity, or the ability of the market to absorb large orders at stable prices, emerged as a significant concern following the collapse of FTX and its sister concern, Alameda. While the order book depth has surely improved for major coins, fragmentation or distribution of liquidity across multiple DeFi platforms, blockchains and networks, remains a concern.

data.title: China CSSC Holdings Notifies Creditors of Merger With China Shipbuilding Industry
data.description:
China CSSC Holdings urged creditors to file claims amid a share-swap merger with China Shipbuilding Industry , according to a Wednesday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Following the merger, China Shipbuilding Industry will delist and cancel its legal person status.

Creditors have 30 days from notification or 45 days from this announcement to request debt repayment or guarantees.

data.title: Kyrgyzstan Inflation at Over 1-Year High
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Kyrgyzstan increased to 6.7% in January 2025, from 6.3% in the previous month.

This marked the highest reading since December 2023, driven by rising costs for food & non-alcoholic beverages (6.1% vs 5.4% in December), health (8.3% vs 7.9%), and transport (8.7% vs 8%).

Meanwhile, inflation slowed for housing and utilities (9.6% vs 10.2%) and restaurants and hotels (4.3% vs 5.1%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices rose by 0.6%, easing from a 0.9% gain in the preceding period.

data.title: Reserve Bank of Australia's 'Hawkish' Tone Post-Easing Supports Case for Next Final Easing in August, Says ANZ Research
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) signaled a "hawkish" stance on Tuesday as it raised caution over further easing following its first rate cut since 2020, ANZ Research said on the same day.

The RBA lowered the official cash rate by 25 basis points to 4.10% on Wednesday in a widely expected move.

The central bank, however, signaled a cautious tone about additional rate cuts amid risks of disrupting disinflation.

"[I]f monetary policy is eased too much too soon, disinflation could stall," according to the RBA's monetary policy statement.

The RBA also revised its forecasts, with gross domestic product (GDP) growth for the December 2024 and June 2025 quarters downgraded to 1.1% and 2%, respectively. GDP growth for the December 2025 quarter is expected at 2.4%, up from 2.3% in the previous forecast.

Inflation is forecast to come in at 2.4% in the December 2024 and June 2025 quarters - also down from recent projections - before accelerating to 3.7% in the final quarter of the year.

"The refreshed forecasts and hawkish statement support our forecast for a shallow easing cycle, with the next cut (and last, in our view) expected in the 11 to 12 August meeting," ANZ Research wrote in its note.

data.title: Cipla Completes Additional Investment in South Africa- Based Wholly-Owned Arm
data.description:
Indian pharmaceutical company Cipla has completed an investment of 900 million South African rands in its South Africa-based wholly-owned subsidiary Cipla Medpro South Africa Proprietary, according to a Tuesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The investment was made against the allotment of 40,742,417 shares of the subsidiary on Tuesday.

Shares of the company were down over 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Market Chatter: Poste Italiane Open to Increasing Telecom Italia Stake Beyond 10%
data.description:
Poste Italiane (PST.MI) plans to increase its stake in Telecom Italia (TIT.MI) to over 10%, Reuters reported Tuesday, citing three unnamed sources.

The Italian state-run postal service recently acquired a 9.8% stake in the telecommunications company from state lender Cassa Depositi e Prestiti, or CDP. The sources said Poste Italiane is exploring all potential options, but no decision has been taken yet.

The stake increase is meant to strengthen Poste Italiane's position in any future deals for Telecom Italia, according to the sources.

Poste Italiane did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Mitsui & Co. to Buy 40% Interest in Australia's Iron Ore Project for $5.34 Billion
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

Mitsui & Co. said it would acquire a 40% interest in Rhodes Ridge iron ore project in Australia for $5.34 billion.

The Japanese trading house said Wednesday that the Rhodes Ridge project will be developed and operated by Rio Tinto.

Production is expected to start by 2030, Mitsui said.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: J&T Express's Southeast Asian Business May Not Grow as Fast as Planned — Market Talk
data.description: J&T Global Express's Southeast Asian business might not grow as fast as the Chinese delivery company expected, Morningstar's Chelsey Tam says in a commentary. The analyst expects the company's Southeast Asia express volume share to increase to 29% by 2028. That compares with J&T Express's goal of quickly increasing its volume market share in Southeast Asia to 40%, she notes. Increasing gross merchandise volume share gains for e-commerce platform Shopee and express volume share gains for its logistics arm, SPX Express, will limit J&T's upside volume share gain potential, Tam says. She keeps a fair-value estimate of HK$6.10 on J&T Express. Share are last at HK$6.54. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: HSBC: Commited to Achieving Annualized Cost Base Reduction of $1.5B by End-2026 >0005.HK
Chinese Equities Could Underperform This Year — Market Talk
data.description: Chinese equities will likely underperform this year despite the recent rally, says Capital Economics senior markets economist James Reilly. Chinese equities have outperformed in recent weeks amid the enthusiasm over artificial intelligence, he says in a note. While the outlook for Chinese equities has improved, the lack of checks and balances in China means that this attitude shift in the tech sector could reverse at a moment's notice, he says. The threat is always likely to weigh on valuations, he adds. Capital Economics remains doubtful that the benefits of AI will diffuse through China's economy. This means that AI-related gains may remain largely concentrated in the tech sector instead of pushing stronger earnings growth across other sectors in China's market.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: HSBC: Expect to Incur Severance, Other Upfront Costs of $1.8B Over 2025, 2026 >0005.HK
Turkey - Factors To Watch on Feb 19
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Wednesday.

The lira traded at 36.30 against the U.S. dollar at 0413 GMT, unchanged from its close on Tuesday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 0.82% higher at 9,916.89 points on Tuesday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian stocks wavered on Wednesday after the S&P 500 and European shares ended at record highs, in the face of U.S President Donald Trump's latest tariff threats on auto, semiconductor and pharmaceutical imports.

ERDOGAN PROGRAMME

President Tayyip Erdogan will address lawmakers and members of his ruling AK Party in parliament. (0900 GMT)

VICE PRESIDENT

Vice President Cevdet Yilmaz will participate in an event on forestry and sustainable living in Ankara. Agriculture and Forestry Minister Ibrahim Yumakli will also attend the event. (0700 GMT)

COMMUNICATIONS DIRECTOR

Turkey's Presidential Communications Director Fahrettin Altun will speak at a panel titled "Turkey's Stabilising Role in Syria's Security" in Ankara. (0630 GMT)

PARLIAMENT SPEAKER

Turkey's Parliament Speaker Numan Kurtulmus will meet with civilian organisations during a trip to Japan.

OPPOSITION

Ozgur Ozel, head of the main opposition Republican People's Party (CHP), will meet with members of his party's provincial council. (0800 GMT)

PARLIAMENTARY MEETINGS

The Yeni Yol (New Road) alliance and IYI Party will hold their parliamentary group meetings. (0630 GMT) (0730 GMT)

ZELENSKIY-ERDOGAN TALKS IN ANKARA

Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy postponed his visit to Saudi Arabia in order to not give "legitimacy" to Tuesday's meeting between U.S. and Russian officials in Riyadh, two sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan said on Tuesday that Ukraine's territorial integrity and sovereignty were indisputable in the eyes of Ankara in the coming period, adding Turkey would be an appropriate venue for any possible peace talks involving Russia.

TUPRAS STOPS BUYING RUSSIAN CRUDE

Turkey's largest oil refiner Tupras has stopped buying Russian crude because of U.S. sanctions announced on January 10 against Russian energy companies and tankers carrying Russian oil, the company said on a call following an earnings report.

IRAQ-TURKEY PIPELINE

A major pipeline connecting Iraq's semi-autonomous Kurdistan region to Turkey is ready to reopen and resume exports, the Kurdish foreign minister said on Tuesday, potentially ending a dispute between Baghdad and Erbil that led to the closure of the pipeline in 2023.

TUSIAD INVESTIGATION

Turkey's main business group TUSIAD defended itself on Tuesday as working for the national interest after an investigation was launched into one of its executive's criticism of a crackdown on dissenting figures.

RAIDS ON PKK SUSPECTS

Turkish police have detained 282 suspects accused of ties to the outlawed Kurdistan Workers Party, or PKK, militant group, the government said on Tuesday, among them journalists, politicians, and academics.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Tuesday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Stocks Edge Higher; Intel Shares Rally

U.S. stocks rose slightly to start the week. Intel shares jumped after The Wall Street Journal reported that Broadcom and TSMC were eyeing separate deals that would break the U.S. chip maker in two.

Trump Floats 25% Tariffs on Autos, Chips, Pharmaceuticals

The president provided further details about his expected tariff moves.

Howard Lutnick Is Confirmed as Commerce Secretary With Tariff Fights Heating Up

The billionaire Wall Street veteran is joining an economic team that includes a trade hawk, Peter Navarro.

China Home Prices Show Slight Improvement But Remain in Decline

Home prices in major Chinese cities continued to slightly narrow their declines in January, suggesting that Beijing's policy blitz has helped stabilize the beleaguered real-estate sector.

BOJ's Takata Calls for Gradual Rate Hikes, Sees Upside Risks to Prices

The Bank of Japan needs to keep adjusting the degree of monetary accommodation in stages, policy board member Hajime Takata said as he warned about inflation risks.

RBNZ Slashes Rates Further, Sounds Global Trade Warning

What does the Fed really think about Trump tariffs and inflation? We're about to get more clues.

Inquiring minds want to know: What do Federal Reserve leaders think about President Trump's tariffs and how they will affect inflation? And how will they react if he imposes stiff sanctions on other countries?

U.S., Russia Forge New Path on Ukraine After First High-Level Talks

Canada Inflation Accelerated Slightly to 1.9% in January

Market expectations for a pause in interest-rate cuts increase after the higher consumer-price report.

German Economic Hopes Rise as Election Nears

Investor confidence rose this month on hopes that a change in government at elections on Feb. 23 could spur a reversal of economic fortunes.

data.title: HSBC: Board Approved Fourth Interim Dividend of 36c/Share >0005.HK
Alibaba Profit, Revenue Likely Rose in Third Quarter — Earnings Preview
data.description:
By Tracy Qu

Alibaba Group is scheduled to report results for its fiscal third quarter on Thursday. Here is what you need to know:

NET PROFIT: The Chinese e-commerce titan is expected to report net profit of 36.26 billion yuan, equivalent to $4.99 billion, for the three months ended December, according to the consensus estimate of 21 analysts polled by FactSet. That would compare with net profit of 14.43 billion yuan a year earlier, when it booked more than $3 billion in impairments related to retail operator Sun Art and the Youku video platform, and 43.87 billion yuan in the September quarter.

REVENUE: Third-quarter revenue was likely 279.03 billion yuan, according to FactSet, up from 260.35 billion yuan in the previous year and 236.50 billion yuan in the previous quarter.

The company's Hong Kong-listed stock declined 25% in the December quarter, in part a correction from sharp gains in late September when China's policy push lifted stocks across the board. Alibaba's shares have since regained momentum, surging more than 50% this year amid the buzz around DeepSeek and AI more broadly.

WHAT TO WATCH:

  • DOMESTIC E-COMMERCE: Alibaba's gross merchandise value and customer management revenue, a metric that tracks how much it makes through merchants, likely improved faster than initial projections, thanks to a decent Singles' Day sales campaign and rising adoption of a new digital marketing tool, Citi analysts led by Alicia Yap said in a research note. Citi projected 5% growth in customer management revenue for the fiscal third quarter, up from 3.7% forecast previously.
  • CLOUD COMPUTING: Alibaba's cloud revenue likely grew 10% during the December quarter, according to HSBC analysts. Investors will also be watching for further developments after the Chinese tech giant's chairman said last week that Apple will use Alibaba's AI technology for iPhones in China.

Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com

data.title: HSBC: Total Dividend Payout for 2024 to Be 87c/Share >0005.HK
Gold falls on profit-taking, hopes for Russia-Ukraine peace talks
data.description:
Key points:
  • Bullion is $14 shy of its $2,942.70 record high
  • Gold's upside remains capped says analyst
  • Fed's January meeting minutes due later

Gold fell on Wednesday on profit-taking after prices hit record highs recently, with investors watching out for peace talks after U.S. President Donald Trump's administration agreed to hold more talks with Russia on ending the war in Ukraine.

Spot gold shed 0.2% to $2,928.52 an ounce, as of 0338 GMT, $14 shy of its all-time high of $2,942.70 hit last week.

U.S. gold futures dropped 0.1% to $2,945.90.

"Gold's upside remains capped as first round of talks between the U.S. and Russia over potential peace deal in Ukraine has ended with no clear path but if they come out with a solid plan, then definitely it could be negative for gold," said Ajay Kedia, director at Mumbai-based Kedia Commodities.

"There should be technical profit booking because war premium should be slightly eroding. The upside could be capped around $2,970 as resistance and $2,890 as support."

Trump's administration said on Tuesday it agreed to hold more talks with Russia on ending the war in Ukraine after the initial Russia-Ukraine peace talks finished without Kyiv or Europe at the table.

Bullion is viewed as a traditional hedge against rising inflation and geopolitical uncertainties.

"Trump's presidency is creating macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainties that are likely to prompt investors to diversify into gold," analysts at ANZ said, adding that investment demand (in gold) would benefit from macroeconomic, geopolitical, trade and fiscal risks.

The bullion rose over 1% in the last session on concerns over economic growth, due to uncertainty surrounding Trump's tariff plans prompting safe-haven flows into bullion.

The market now awaits the Federal Reserve's January meeting minutes due later in the day for clues into the U.S. central bank's interest rate trajectory this year.

Spot silver dipped 0.9% to $32.57 an ounce. Platinum fell 1.3% to $974.32, and palladium was 1.3% lower at $974.56.

data.title: DIIs pour in over Rs 1 lakh crore in less than two months of 2025 even as FIIs pull out over $12 billion
data.description:
Domestic institutional investors (DIIs), which had a record year in terms of net buying in 2024, are continuing their aggressive buying in the current calendar year with the cumulative buying already crossing the Rs 1 lakh crore mark even as their foreign counterparts are selling Indian shares in huge quantum.

According to NSE data, DIIs have invested Rs 1.2 lakh crore in equities since January, whereas FIIs have offloaded Indian stocks worth almost a similar amount –Rs 1.06 lakh crore. Last year, DIIs were net buyers at over Rs 5.22 lakh crore in equities, marking a record high, even as FIIs ended the year as net sellers at Rs 427 crore.

While domestic inflows have provided some support during the ongoing volatility, both the benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty have declined over 3 percent each so far this year, while the broader markets, represented by the BSE MidCap and BSE SmallCap indices have plummeted more than 20 percent each.

Brokerage firm Jefferies, in its latest report, has cautioned that the steady inflow of retail funds into India’s equity mutual funds is at risk of slowing as market returns weaken. A potential decline in household investments in domestic equities could further weigh on Asia’s fourth-largest equity market, which has been in a downward trend since late September due to an ongoing earnings slowdown and persistent foreign fund outflows, it said.

Meanwhile, experts believe that going ahead while DIIs may moderate their pace of buying in case of prolonged weakness they are unlikely to stop investing altogether. Markets are now approaching their long-term PE averages, making valuations more attractive.

Additionally, steady SIP inflows of over Rs 25,000 crore per month ensure that DIIs have a continuous liquidity pipeline. Institutional preference for Indian equities remains high due to stable macroeconomic conditions and limited alternatives, while earnings growth in key sectors like banking, auto, and consumption further supports continued allocations, say experts.

Narinder Wadhwa, Managing Director & CEO of SKI Capital services said in the near term, markets may remain volatile due to FII selling, global uncertainties such as US Federal Reserve policy, geopolitical risks, and Trump’s recent tariffs. However, strong support is expected around the 22,300-22,500 levels on the Nifty, where fresh buying could emerge. Medium-term recovery prospects remain intact, driven by RBI’s rate cuts, robust domestic demand, and long-term economic growth drivers, he said.

Experts further add that sectoral rotation will play a crucial role, with banking, consumption, and infrastructure likely to outperform, while high-valuation sectors like IT and new-age tech may face pressure. Overall, while short-term corrections are possible, DIIs are expected to continue their support, preventing a deep market correction unless a major external shock occurs.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before making any investment decisions.
